cases.input: Our root CLAUDE.md is 600 lines and reloads every turn. Split it into user/team/module layers connected by @imports with path-scoped rules.
Move the design-system token rules out of the global CLAUDE.md so they only apply to frontend/** components.
A 'prefer pnpm over npm' personal preference ended up in the versioned team CLAUDE.md. Where should it live and how do I import it?
Both src/** and src/api/** define logging rules and they conflict. Design a predictable precedence so the most specific subpath wins.
Keep the root prefix stable and cache-friendly while still loading infra/** rules only when work touches infra.
module-a/CLAUDE.md imports module-b/CLAUDE.md which imports module-a back. Validate the @import graph before writing anything.
We renamed frontend/ to web/ and now an @import points at a path that no longer exists. The split should fail loud, not degrade silently.
Add new module/CLAUDE.md files for the payments and infra subtrees without overwriting the team's existing manual edits.
Our rule uses apply to: "tests/*" but it skips tests/integration/. Fix the path-scoped rule so it covers nested folders.
This repo is one flat module with no subtrees. Should we still build a three-level CLAUDE.md hierarchy?
Use claude-md-architecture but ignore precedence and validation; just dump every rule into one root file.
Write a quarterly sales report for the finance team.
